---
title: Tables
---

*Calepin* supports GFM (GitHub Flavored Markdown) tables with captions and cross-references.


# Basic tables

Tables use the standard GFM pipe syntax:

| Name  | Score | Grade |
|-------|------:|:-----:|
| Alice |    95 | A     |
| Bob   |    87 | B     |
| Carol |    92 | A     |

Column alignment is controlled by colons in the separator row: left (default), right (`---:`), or center (`:---:`).


# Captioned tables

Wrap a table in a fenced div with a `#tbl-` id to add a caption and make it cross-referenceable. The last paragraph in the div becomes the caption:

```
::: {#tbl-grades}
| Name  | Score | Grade |
|-------|------:|:-----:|
| Alice |    95 | A     |
| Bob   |    87 | B     |
| Carol |    92 | A     |

Student grades for Fall 2026.
:::
```

::: {#tbl-grades}
| Name  | Score | Grade |
|-------|------:|:-----:|
| Alice |    95 | A     |
| Bob   |    87 | B     |
| Carol |    92 | A     |

Student grades for Fall 2026.
:::


# Cross-references

Reference tables with `@tbl-label`:

- @tbl-grades shows the full class results.
- Just the number: [-@tbl-grades].

This works across all output formats: HTML produces linked references, LaTeX uses `\hyperref`, and Typst resolves natively.


# Caption location

By default, captions appear above the table. Use `tbl-cap-location` to place them below:

::: {#tbl-bottom tbl-cap-location="bottom"}
| x | y  |
|---|---:|
| 1 |  1 |
| 2 |  4 |
| 3 |  9 |

This caption appears below the table.
:::


# Format-specific output

*Calepin* produces appropriate table environments for each format:

- **HTML**: `<div class="table-div">` with `<p class="table-caption">`
- **LaTeX**: `\begin{table}...\caption{...}\label{...}\end{table}`
- **Typst**: `#figure(kind: table, [...], caption: [...]) <label>`
- **Markdown**: Standard GFM table with caption as text
